entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop used in the study for phenotyping and genotypic discretization.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RESTRICTION
entities.description: The ability of a plant to limit water loss under high vapor pressure deficit.

entities.name: VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: The difference between the amount of moisture in the air and how much moisture the air can hold when it is saturated.

entities.name: WATER-SAVING TRAIT
entities.description: A trait that allows plants to conserve water, essential for drought adaptation.

entities.name: DROUGHT ADAPTATION
entities.description: The process by which a plant adjusts to survive and function under water-limited conditions.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: A method for rapidly measuring many traits across a large population of plants.

entities.name: MAXIMUM TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The highest rate at which a plant loses water through transpiration.

entities.name: VPD BREAKPOINTS
entities.description: The specific vapor pressure deficit levels at which genotypes begin to restrict transpiration.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION PROFILES
entities.description: Smooth representations of transpiration rates over time derived from high-frequency data.

entities.name: LOAD CELLS
entities.description: Sensors used to measure the weight of plants to derive transpiration rate profiles.

entities.name: UNSUPERVISED RANDOM FOREST
entities.description: A machine learning algorithm used for feature selection and clustering of genotypes.

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES
entities.description: Non-cultivated ancestors or relatives of the chickpea crop.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of carbon gain to water loss.

entities.name: MULTI-OUTPUT NEURAL NETWORK
entities.description: A deep learning model used for sensitivity analysis of water saving ability.

entities.name: WATER SAVING ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a genotype to restrict transpiration and conserve water.

entities.name: LIGHT
entities.description: Environmental factor that had little to no effect on transpiration response independently.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental factor that had little to no effect on transpiration response independently.

entities.name: RELATIVE HUMIDITY
entities.description: Environmental factor that had little to no effect on transpiration response independently.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and yield under drought stress.

entities.name: PRESCRIPTIVE BREEDING
entities.description: An approach to breeding that uses precise data to select for specific traits.
